name: paso-de-la-amada-ballcourt
description: The earliest known formal ballcourt was built at Paso de la Amada in Chiapas, Mexico, launching the Mesoamerican ballgame.

name: wrestling-pentathlon-added-708-bce
description: Wrestling and the five-event pentathlon joined the ancient Olympic program at Olympia, broadening it beyond footraces.

name: boxing-added-olympics-688-bce
description: Boxing, or pygmachia, was added to the ancient Olympic Games at Olympia, with fighters binding their hands in leather thongs.

name: tethrippon-chariot-race-680-bce
description: The four-horse chariot race, the tethrippon, debuted at Olympia as the ancient Games' first equestrian event.

name: pythian-games-crown-582-bce
description: The Pythian Games at Delphi, honoring Apollo, were reorganized as a four-yearly crown festival with athletic contests added.

name: milo-croton-wrestling-reign-532-bce
description: Milo of Croton won the first of five straight Olympic men's wrestling crowns, becoming antiquity's most famous athlete.

name: cuju-earliest-football
description: Cuju, a kicking ball game recognized by FIFA as football's ancestor, is documented in China's Warring States era.

name: first-roman-gladiator-munus-264-bce
description: The sons of Brutus Pera staged Rome's first gladiatorial combat, three pairs of fighters, as a funeral rite in the Forum Boarium.

name: leonidas-rhodes-twelve-crowns-152-bce
description: Leonidas of Rhodes finished a fourth Olympiad sweep of three footraces, amassing a record twelve individual crowns.

name: pompeii-amphitheatre-built-70-bce
description: Magistrates Valgus and Porcius built the amphitheatre at Pompeii, the oldest surviving Roman stone arena for gladiatorial games.

name: colosseum-inaugural-games-80-ce
description: Emperor Titus opened the Colosseum with 100 days of gladiatorial fights, wild-beast hunts, and staged spectacles in Rome.

name: ancient-olympics-abolished-393-ce
description: Emperor Theodosius I's ban on pagan festivals is traditionally credited with ending the ancient Olympic Games.

name: nika-riots-constantinople-532-ce
description: Constantinople's Blue and Green chariot factions united in revolt against Justinian, killing tens of thousands and burning the city.

name: kemari-earliest-record
description: Japan's Nihon Shoki records Prince Naka no Oe and Nakatomi no Kamatari meeting at a kemari football game.

name: sechie-zumo-imperial-court
description: Emperor Shomu staged formal palace sumo tournaments, launching the sechie-zumo court ritual in Nara Japan.

name: first-recorded-tournament-valenciennes-1114
description: The word 'tournament' first appears in Count Baldwin III of Hainaut's 1114 peace charter for the town of Valenciennes.

name: yabusame-established-yoritomo
description: Shogun Minamoto no Yoritomo instituted yabusame horseback archery as a Shinto rite at Tsurugaoka Hachimangu.

name: genghis-khan-stele-archery
description: A Mongolian stele recorded Yesungge's archery shot of over 500 meters at a gathering of Genghis Khan's nobles.

name: southampton-oldest-bowling-green-1299
description: The Old Bowling Green in Southampton, England, hosted its first recorded game of bowls in 1299 and is still played on today.

name: edward-ii-bans-football-london-1314
friendly_name: Edward II Bans Football in London
description: King Edward II banned football within the City of London in 1314, citing the tumult and brawling caused by mob football games.

name: kirkpinar-oil-wrestling-founded
description: The Ottoman Kırkpınar tournament in Edirne began, becoming the world's oldest continuous sanctioned sport.

name: edward-iii-archery-law-1363
description: King Edward III's 1363 decree ordered every able-bodied Englishman to practise archery on Sundays and forbade rival sports.

name: scotland-football-act-1424
description: Scotland's Parliament under King James I passed the Football Act of 1424, fining anyone caught playing football fourpence.

name: calcio-fiorentino-siege-match-1530
description: Florentines defiantly played calcio in the Piazza Santa Croce in 1530 while the city was under siege by Emperor Charles V's troops.

name: falkland-oldest-real-tennis-court-1539
description: King James V built the real tennis court at Falkland Palace, Scotland, in 1539, now the oldest surviving court in the world.

name: chester-races-roodee
description: Horse racing began at Chester's Roodee course in 1539, recognised by Guinness as the oldest racecourse still in operation.

name: henry-ii-france-jousting-death-1559
description: King Henry II of France was fatally wounded when a lance splinter pierced his eye during a Paris joust in 1559.

name: bardi-calcio-fiorentino-rules-1580
description: Florentine count Giovanni de' Bardi published the first codified rules of calcio fiorentino, set out in 33 articles, in 1580.

name: naqsh-e-jahan-polo-ground
description: Shah Abbas laid out Isfahan's Naqsh-e Jahan Square as a polo field; its stone goalposts still stand today.

name: earliest-cricket-reference-guildford
description: A 1598 court case in Guildford recorded testimony that cricket, or 'creckett', had been played there by schoolboys around 1550.

name: newmarket-town-plate-founded
description: King Charles II set out the rules for the Newmarket Town Plate in 1665, one of the first horse races run under a formal code.

name: james-figg-first-boxing-champion
description: James Figg opened his London amphitheatre in 1719 and became recognised as England's first bare-knuckle boxing champion.

name: broughtons-rules-boxing
description: English champion Jack Broughton drew up boxing's first written code in 1743, introducing the count and banning hits to a downed man.

name: first-rules-of-golf-leith
description: The Gentlemen Golfers of Edinburgh drew up golf's first 13 written rules in 1744 for a competition on Leith Links.

name: laws-of-cricket-codified-1744
description: The London Cricket Club at the Artillery Ground drew up the earliest known code of the Laws of Cricket, fixing the 22-yard pitch.

name: society-of-st-andrews-golfers
description: Formed in 1754, the Society of St Andrews Golfers later became the Royal and Ancient, golf's global rule-making authority.

name: first-oxford-cambridge-boat-race
description: Oxford beat Cambridge at Henley-on-Thames in the first University Boat Race, launching one of sport's oldest rivalries.

name: first-henley-regatta
description: The inaugural Henley Regatta was held on the Thames at Henley-on-Thames, later gaining royal patronage from Prince Albert in 1851.

name: first-americas-cup-race
description: The schooner America won the Royal Yacht Squadron's race around the Isle of Wight, originating the America's Cup trophy.

name: aussie-rules-first-laws-1859
description: Tom Wills and colleagues drafted the ten original Rules of the Melbourne Football Club, founding Australian rules football.

name: first-open-championship-1860
description: Willie Park Sr won the inaugural Open at Prestwick in Scotland, beating Tom Morris Sr among a field of eight golfers.

name: queensberry-rules-published-1867
description: The glove-mandating Marquess of Queensberry Rules, drafted by John Graham Chambers, were published in London.

name: first-college-football-game-1869
description: In New Brunswick, Rutgers beat Princeton 6-4 in the first intercollegiate American football game, played under rugby-like rules.

name: cincinnati-red-stockings-first-pro-team-1869
description: Harry Wright's Cincinnati Red Stockings became baseball's first openly all-salaried team, touring the nation undefeated.

name: rugby-football-union-founded-1871
description: Representatives of 21 clubs met at London's Pall Mall Restaurant to found the Rugby Football Union and codify its laws.

name: first-rugby-international-scotland-england
description: Scotland beat England at Raeburn Place in Edinburgh in the world's first international rugby match, played 20-a-side.

name: first-football-international-1872
description: Scotland and England drew 0-0 at Hamilton Crescent in Glasgow in the first officially recognised international match.

name: wingfield-patents-lawn-tennis
description: Major Walter Clopton Wingfield patented his portable court game 'Sphairistike' in England, launching lawn tennis.

name: webb-first-english-channel-swim-1875
description: Captain Matthew Webb became the first person to swim the English Channel unaided, from Dover to Calais.

name: snooker-invented-chamberlain-india
description: British officer Neville Chamberlain devised snooker at an army mess in Jabalpur, India, blending pyramids and black pool.

name: national-league-founded-1876
description: William Hulbert founded the National League of Professional Base Ball Clubs, still one of Major League Baseball's two leagues.

name: first-us-national-tennis-championship
description: The inaugural U.S. National Championship was played at the Newport Casino in Rhode Island, the event that became the US Open.

name: the-ashes-born-1882
description: After Australia's first Test win on English soil at The Oval, a mock obituary in the Sporting Times gave birth to the Ashes.

name: jigoro-kano-founds-judo-1882
description: Jigoro Kano founded Kodokan judo in Tokyo, opening his first dojo at Eishoji Temple with nine students.

name: axel-paulsen-invents-axel-jump
description: Norwegian Axel Paulsen debuted his forward-takeoff jump in Vienna, creating the axel, figure skating's oldest jump.

name: maud-watson-first-wimbledon-ladies-1884
description: Maud Watson beat her sister Lilian in 1884 to win Wimbledon's first Ladies' Singles, tennis's first women's major title.

name: gaelic-athletic-association-founded-1884
description: Michael Cusack and others founded the GAA at Hayes' Hotel in Thurles to revive hurling and Gaelic football in Ireland.

name: football-league-founded-1888
description: Aston Villa's William McGregor founded the Football League with twelve clubs, the world's first organised football league.

name: naismith-basketball-rules-published
description: James Naismith's original 13 rules of basketball were published in the Springfield YMCA journal The Triangle in January 1892.

name: first-gloved-heavyweight-title-1892
description: James J. Corbett knocked out John L. Sullivan in New Orleans to win boxing's first gloved world heavyweight title.

name: first-motor-race-paris-rouen-1894
description: Le Petit Journal's Paris-to-Rouen contest in France is widely regarded as the world's first competitive motor race.

name: great-schism-northern-union-1895
description: Twenty-one northern clubs met at Huddersfield's George Hotel and broke from the RFU, founding rugby league's forerunner.

name: first-us-open-golf-1895
description: Englishman Horace Rawlins won the inaugural U.S. Open at Newport Golf Club in Rhode Island, staged by the USGA.

name: first-modern-olympics-athens-1896
description: The first modern Olympic Games opened in Athens, with 241 male athletes from 14 nations competing.

name: cycling-olympic-debut-athens-1896
description: Cycling featured at the first modern Olympics in Athens, with six track and road events at the Neo Phaliron Velodrome.

name: first-paris-roubaix-1896-fischer
description: Germany's Josef Fischer won the inaugural Paris-Roubaix, the 280 km classic later nicknamed the Hell of the North.

name: first-davis-cup-tennis
description: The United States beat the British Isles in Boston in the first international team tennis competition, founded by Dwight Davis.

name: uci-founded-1900-paris
description: The Union Cycliste Internationale, cycling's world governing body, was founded in Paris by five national federations.

name: women-first-compete-olympics-1900
description: Women competed at the Olympics for the first time in Paris, where Charlotte Cooper became the first female champion.

name: rowing-olympic-debut-1900
description: Rowing was contested at the Paris Olympics after the 1896 races were cancelled by weather, marking its first Olympic appearance.

name: first-bodybuilding-contest-1901
description: Eugen Sandow staged 'The Great Competition' at London's Royal Albert Hall, regarded as the first modern bodybuilding show.

name: forward-pass-legalized-1906
description: College rules makers legalized the forward pass to open up play and curb the deadly mass formations causing fatalities.

name: first-french-grand-prix-1906
description: The Automobile Club de France ran the first race titled Grand Prix on closed roads near Le Mans, won by Ferenc Szisz.

name: fina-founded-1908
description: Eight national federations founded FINA during the London Olympics to govern swimming, diving and water polo.

name: first-giro-ditalia-1909-ganna
description: Luigi Ganna won the first Giro d'Italia, an eight-stage race launched by La Gazzetta dello Sport starting in Milan.

name: first-monte-carlo-rally-1911
description: Henri Rougier won the first Monte Carlo Rally, driving a Turcat-Méry to Monaco from one of eleven European start cities.

name: iaaf-founded-stockholm-1912
description: Delegates from 17 national federations met after the Stockholm Olympics to found the IAAF, athletics' world governing body.

name: first-olympic-decathlon-thorpe
description: The ten-event decathlon debuted at the Stockholm Olympics, won by American Jim Thorpe with a score that stood for years.

name: olympic-rings-designed-1913
description: Pierre de Coubertin designed the five interlocking Olympic rings, symbolizing the union of the world's continents.

name: first-pga-championship-1916
description: Jim Barnes won the first PGA Championship at Siwanoy Country Club the same year the PGA of America was founded.

name: nfl-founded-canton-1920
description: The American Professional Football Association, renamed the NFL in 1922, formed in Ralph Hay's Canton, Ohio auto showroom.

name: negro-national-league-founded-1920
description: Rube Foster founded the Negro National League in Kansas City, the first lasting professional league for Black ballplayers.

name: first-prix-arc-de-triomphe
friendly_name: First Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe
description: The Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe was first run at Longchamp in Paris in 1920, won by Comrade, becoming Europe's premier race.

name: first-24-hours-le-mans-1923
description: André Lagache and René Léonard won the inaugural 24 Hours of Le Mans endurance race in a Chenard-Walcker in France.

name: harlem-globetrotters-founded
description: Abe Saperstein founded the Harlem Globetrotters, an all-Black touring exhibition team, in Chicago in 1926.

name: ederle-first-woman-channel-swim-1926
description: American Gertrude Ederle became the first woman to swim the English Channel, beating the men's record.

name: first-ryder-cup-1927
description: The United States, captained by Walter Hagen, beat Great Britain at Worcester Country Club in the first Ryder Cup.

name: women-first-olympic-athletics-1928
description: Women competed in Olympic track and field for the first time at Amsterdam, where Lina Radke won the debut 800m in a world record.

name: sonja-henie-first-olympic-gold
description: Norway's Sonja Henie won her first Olympic gold at age 15 in St. Moritz, transforming ladies' skating with balletic style.

name: first-monaco-grand-prix-1929
description: William Grover-Williams won the first Monaco Grand Prix in a Bugatti T35B on the streets of Monte Carlo.

name: bobby-jones-grand-slam-1930
description: Amateur Bobby Jones won all four majors of his era in one calendar year, golf's only single-season Grand Slam.

name: first-masters-tournament-1934
description: Horton Smith won the inaugural Masters at Augusta National, the Georgia course co-founded by Bobby Jones.

name: trampoline-invented-nissen-1934
description: American gymnast George Nissen and coach Larry Griswold built the first modern trampoline from iron, canvas and rubber.

name: first-heisman-trophy-1935
description: Chicago halfback Jay Berwanger won the first Downtown Athletic Club Trophy, renamed the Heisman Trophy the next year.

name: first-vuelta-a-espana-1935-deloor
description: Belgian Gustaaf Deloor won the inaugural Vuelta a España, a 14-stage race finishing in Madrid.

name: jesse-owens-four-golds-1936
description: American Jesse Owens won four gold medals at the Berlin Olympics, defying Nazi theories of Aryan racial supremacy.

name: basketball-olympic-debut-1936
description: Basketball debuted as an Olympic medal event at the 1936 Berlin Games, with the USA winning gold on an outdoor court.

name: alpine-skiing-olympic-debut-1936
description: Alpine skiing debuted at the Olympics at Garmisch-Partenkirchen in 1936 with a combined downhill and slalom event.

name: first-olympic-torch-relay-1936
description: The Berlin Games introduced the Olympic torch relay, carrying a flame lit in Olympia, Greece, to the host stadium.

name: nba-formed-baa-nbl-merger
description: The NBA was created on August 3, 1949, when the Basketball Association of America merged with the National Basketball League.

name: first-f1-world-championship-race-1950
description: Nino Farina won the first Formula One World Championship race at Silverstone in an Alfa Romeo, watched by King George VI.

name: nba-24-second-shot-clock-introduced
description: The NBA introduced Danny Biasone's 24-second shot clock for the 1954-55 season, transforming the pace of the game.

name: taekwondo-is-named-1955
description: General Choi Hong Hi coined the name taekwondo in South Korea, unifying Korean kicking-and-striking martial arts.

name: first-european-cup-1956
description: Real Madrid beat Stade de Reims 4-3 in Paris to win the first European Cup, Europe's premier club competition.

name: afl-founded-lamar-hunt-1959
description: Lamar Hunt founded the American Football League with eight teams to rival the NFL, beginning play in the 1960 season.

name: judo-olympic-debut-tokyo-1964
description: Judo made its Olympic debut in Tokyo, where Dutchman Anton Geesink upset the hosts to win the open weight gold.

name: black-power-salute-mexico-1968
description: Tommie Smith and John Carlos raised black-gloved fists on the Mexico City podium to protest racial injustice.

name: afl-nfl-merger-1970
description: The AFL and NFL completed their merger into one league split into two conferences, the AFC and NFC, for the 1970 season.

name: first-one-day-international-1971
description: After a washed-out Test, Australia beat England at Melbourne in the first One Day International, watched by 46,000 fans.

name: golf-played-on-the-moon-1971
description: Apollo 14 astronaut Alan Shepard hit two golf balls on the lunar surface, the first time golf was played off Earth.

name: battle-of-the-sexes-king-riggs-1973
description: Billie Jean King beat Bobby Riggs in straight sets before some 90 million viewers in the 1973 Battle of the Sexes tennis match.

name: hank-aaron-715th-home-run-1974
description: Atlanta's Hank Aaron hit his 715th home run off Al Downing, surpassing Babe Ruth as baseball's career home run king.

name: first-cricket-world-cup-1975
description: West Indies beat Australia at Lord's to win the first men's Cricket World Cup, the Prudential Cup, with Clive Lloyd scoring 102.

name: comaneci-first-perfect-ten-1976
description: At the Montreal Olympics, Romania's Nadia Comaneci scored the first perfect 10 in Olympic gymnastics on the uneven bars.

name: first-rugby-world-cup-1987
description: New Zealand's All Blacks beat France in Auckland to win the first Rugby World Cup, co-hosted by New Zealand and Australia.

name: first-womens-world-cup-1991
description: The United States beat Norway 2-1 in China to win the inaugural FIFA Women's World Cup football tournament.

name: short-track-olympic-debut-1992
description: Short track speed skating made its official Olympic debut at the Albertville Winter Games with four medal events.

name: first-ufc-event-1993
description: UFC 1 took place in Denver, Colorado, where Brazilian Royce Gracie won the no-rules eight-man tournament.

name: first-extreme-games-1995-rhode-island
description: ESPN staged the first Extreme Games in Rhode Island, launching the action-sports showcase later renamed the X Games.

name: wnba-inaugural-season-1997
description: The Women's National Basketball Association tipped off its first season in 1997, launching a lasting US women's pro league.

name: italy-joins-six-nations-2000
description: Italy joined the Five Nations to create the Six Nations Championship, beating Scotland in their debut match in Rome.

name: taekwondo-becomes-olympic-sport-2000
description: Taekwondo became a full Olympic medal sport at the Sydney Games, with eight weight divisions for men and women.

name: first-twenty20-match-2003
description: Hampshire beat Sussex at the Rose Bowl in the first Twenty20 match, launching England's inaugural Twenty20 Cup competition.

name: first-kabaddi-world-cup-india
description: India beat Iran 55-27 in Mumbai to win the inaugural Kabaddi World Cup, cementing its dominance of the international game.

name: bolt-100m-world-record-958
description: Usain Bolt clocked 9.58 seconds at the Berlin World Championships, setting the 100m world record that still stands.

name: womens-olympic-boxing-debut-2012
description: Women's boxing debuted at the London Olympics, where Britain's Nicola Adams won the first female Olympic gold.
